questions.answer: all over the world.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "The office is cleaned ___"
questions.answer: by a team every morning.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "These phones are made ___"
questions.answer: in factories in Asia.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "The rules are explained ___"
questions.answer: at the beginning of every class.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Fresh bread is baked ___"
questions.answer: here every day.

questions.prompt: Spanish _____ (speak) by over 500 million people around the world.
questions.answer: is spoken — Passive: is/are + past participle. 'Spanish' is singular → 'is spoken'.

questions.prompt: The packages _____ (deliver) every Tuesday and Thursday.
questions.answer: are delivered — Passive: 'the packages' is plural → 'are delivered'.

questions.prompt: These products _____ (not sell) in supermarkets — you can only buy them online.
questions.answer: are not sold — Negative passive: is/are + not + past participle. 'These products' is plural → 'are not sold'.

questions.prompt: _____ these shoes made in Italy or somewhere else?
questions.answer: Are — Question passive: Are/Is + subject + past participle? 'These shoes' is plural → 'Are'.

questions.prompt: The results _____ (announce) at the end of the ceremony every year.
questions.answer: are announced — Passive: 'the results' is plural → 'are announced'.

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "English is speak in many countries around the world."
questions.answer: Use the past participle, not the base form: 'English is spoken in many countries.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "The product is maked by hand in a small workshop."
questions.answer: 'Make' is irregular — the past participle is 'made': 'The product is made by hand.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "Fresh coffee are served here every morning before nine."
questions.answer: 'Fresh coffee' is uncountable/singular → use 'is': 'Fresh coffee is served here every morning.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "These cars is built in a factory by hundreds of workers."
questions.answer: 'These cars' is plural → use 'are': 'These cars are built in a factory.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "The exam papers are collected by the teacher and then keeped in a safe place."
questions.answer: 'Keep' is irregular — the past participle is 'kept': 'and then kept in a safe place.'
English is spoken here.
The office is cleaned every day.
Are these products sold here?
Tickets are not sold at the door.
This cheese is made by hand.
